*Section: THE BOOK OF PURPOSE: What's Your Message?*

---

##    11\. Day 2: The Culture Questions 

You're going to need to understand your friend's connection with culture. What they love about art and culture will be accented WITHOUT looking at what they dislike.

Your friend spends so much time already, obsessing about what they dislike about culture, without realizing that this obsession robs them of so much of their energy and power.

Any time they move towards what they dislike (which is so much easier to focus on), listen but gently nudge them back to focusing on what they love. 

Realign your friend with their positive influences.

Everything they love constitutes the "perfect world" that they'd like to be a part of. Their message is related to this deeply. This is what they want more of in their lives.

By asking these questions they will be thinking about "message". We choose are favorite things because of what they mean to us.

You should write out the answers to the why's. Don't write the artwork down. The art your friend loves is a reflection on something deeper. Focus on that by removing the source of the feeling.

NOTE: Only ask ONE question at a time and make sure you have answers you fully understand.

Don't let them bypass and say they can't think of one. They will.

  


> **1\. What is one of your favorite books of all time? Get them to tell give you a few names.**

> **What's it about? Make sure you understand the response.**

> **Why do you like this book so much? (THIS IS WHAT YOU WRITE DOWN)**

> **(Do this 3X minimum with 3 different books)**

>   
> 

> **2\. What is one of your favorite movies of all time?**

> **What is this movie about? (Make sure you understand the response.)**

> **Why do you like this movie so much? (THIS IS WHAT YOU WRITE DOWN)**

> **(Do this 3X minimum with 3 different movies)**

>   
> 

> **3\. Who is one of your favorite artists of all time?**

> **What are they all about? (Make sure you understand the response.)**

> **Why do you connect with this artist so much? (THIS IS WHAT YOU WRITE DOWN)**

> **(Do this 3X minimum with 3 different artists)**

>   
> 

  


For the next two questions, write out the message. Not the person who has it.

> **4\. Who on instagram has a message you like?**

> **What is their message?**

> **How do they express it? (You're looking for examples of their message)**

> **What do you like about their message so much? (THIS IS WHAT YOU WRITE DOWN)**

>   
> 

> **5\. Who out there has a message you like? (Alive.) Someone that's NOT based on Instagram.**

> **What is their message?**

> **What do you like about their message? (THIS IS WHAT YOU WRITE DOWN)**

  


Repeat out loud every thing you wrote down. Make sure that they agree with everything you wrote.

_Ask them "What do all of these things have in common?"_

Map it out for fun. You're the one with the pen and paper... 

Help your friend make a Venn Diagram of the reasons why you love these things in culture, where do they seem to overlap? What themes come up over and over?

The chances that your friend's message and what they'd like to convey in their art or business is in the realm of all these words on paper... Are really high.

  


[ Complete and Continue   ]()

Also a very pleasant side-effect of doing these questions is that my friend that I'm doing it with is telling people about this site and now they're very curious and interested, and they will ask me questions about what I've learned and I turn into a zealot, heehee

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/14160161/comments/15896543)

I found this one to be more intense than the previous day. It felt much longer even though there were "fewer" questions. Again, many of my favorite things remind me of my childhood. A lot of warm, fuzzy feelings and a sense of wonder. When we went over the things they have in common, the lightbulb started flickering. It's not fully *on* yet, but I can FEEL that it' almost there! Ready for the next part!

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)
